refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability ≥ 0.999 (99.9%) over the agreed evaluation period, measured as the ratio of time the bidirectional command-and-telemetry link is operational to total operational time.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-010 addresses a critical operational requirement for remote equipment control. Refinement clarifies the measurement basis (bidirectional link), acceptance threshold (≥0.999), and evaluation scope (agreed period). High safety criticality demands explicit availability targets to prevent loss-of-control scenarios.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Continuous monitoring of link state transitions (up/down) with timestamped logs; calculation of availability as (total_uptime / total_evaluation_period); comparison against 0.999 threshold; root-cause analysis of any downtime events.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall detect link loss within [X] milliseconds.
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall initiate safe state transition upon link loss.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall maintain network redundancy to achieve ≥0.999 availability.
FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall log and report link availability metrics to the Safety Services module.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement dual independent network paths (primary + backup) with automatic failover; monitor link health continuously; log availability metrics.
DP-B: Implement single primary network path with periodic health checks and manual failover capability; log availability metrics.
DP-C: Implement triple-redundant network paths with voting logic; continuous health monitoring; automatic failover with no operator intervention.
DP-D: Implement primary network path with secondary satellite/cellular backup; automatic failover triggered by link quality degradation; continuous telemetry logging.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Implement dual independent network paths (primary + backup) with automatic failover; monitor link health continuously; log availability metrics.

potential_sources: Link health monitoring logic couples to Telemetry interface (outbound availability data).
Failover logic couples to Tasking interface (must preserve command continuity during switchover).
Safety state transition couples to SafetyAlert interface (must notify Safety Services of link loss events).
coupling_check.notes: Couplings are necessary and manageable via well-defined interfaces. DP-A avoids over-specification (DP-C) while meeting 0.999 target with proven dual-path architecture. DP-B insufficient for high safety criticality. DP-D introduces external dependency (satellite) increasing cost and complexity without proportional availability gain in typical worksite scenarios.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Two independent network paths can be provisioned at the worksite (e.g., two cellular carriers or wired + wireless).
Assumption: Automatic failover logic can be implemented with <100ms switchover latency.
Risk: If both paths fail simultaneously, availability target cannot be met; mitigation requires redundancy analysis and path independence verification.
Risk: Failover logic bugs could cause false positives (unnecessary switchovers) degrading operator experience; mitigation requires extensive testing.
Risk: Link health monitoring overhead may impact real-time telemetry throughput; mitigation requires bandwidth budgeting.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to explicitly specify: (1) path independence criteria (e.g., different carriers, different physical routes), (2) failover latency budget (e.g., <100ms), (3) health check frequency and timeout thresholds, (4) logging granularity (e.g., per-event vs. periodic summaries).

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Owns the dual-path network architecture, failover logic, link health monitoring, and availability metric collection. Responsible for maintaining the bidirectional command-and-telemetry link and reporting link state to Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands from tele-operator; Teleoperation module must preserve command continuity during failover events.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits vehicle state, sensor data, and link availability metrics to tele-operator and monitoring systems.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Notifies Safety Services module of link loss events, failover events, and availability threshold violations.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Abstracts the dual physical network paths (primary and backup); Teleoperation module manages path selection and failover logic above this interface.

module_containment_scores.notes: Teleoperation is the natural owner of link availability logic. Dual-path failover, health monitoring, and availability metrics are core teleoperation concerns. Minor diffusion: SafetyAlert coupling requires coordination with Safety Services (acceptable via interface).

Safety Services should react to link loss (via SafetyAlert) but should not own the link availability mechanism itself. Placing DP-A in Safety Services would scatter network/failover logic away from teleoperation, violating cohesion.

Vehicle module owns vehicle-side hardware and sensors. Link availability is a system-level concern, not vehicle-specific. Placing DP-A in Vehicle would couple vehicle logic to network architecture, reducing reusability.

NGHE Solution is the top-level system container. While it could theoretically own link availability, delegating to Teleoperation (the subsystem responsible for remote operation) is more cohesive and maintainable.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the optimal owner of DP-A (dual-path failover architecture). It has the highest containment score (0.95) and lowest diffusion score (0.05). The module's primary responsibility is maintaining the command-and-telemetry link; link availability is a direct extension of this responsibility. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and manageable.
containment_risks: Risk: If worksite network infrastructure is managed by a separate team, ownership of Network Link abstraction may be ambiguous; mitigation: establish clear interface contract and ownership boundaries.

Risk: Failover logic complexity may grow if additional redundancy strategies (e.g., load balancing, path quality metrics) are added later; mitigation: design Teleoperation module with extensibility in mind.
Risk: Availability metric logging may generate large data volumes; mitigation: implement log rotation and compression strategies within Teleoperation module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that Teleoperation module has authority over Network Link interface and can implement failover logic without external dependencies. Establish clear SLA between Teleoperation and network infrastructure provider (if external).

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (dual-path failover) directly addresses FR-NGHE-010 (achieve ≥0.999 availability). The DP is specific, measurable, and achievable. Dual-path architecture with automatic failover is a proven pattern for high-availability systems. Assumptions and risks are documented and manageable.

diffusion_risks: Failover logic must coordinate with Tasking interface to preserve command continuity; this is a necessary coupling, not a diffusion risk.
SafetyAlert coupling requires Teleoperation to notify Safety Services of link events; this is a clean interface dependency, not diffusion.
Network Link abstraction must be stable; if underlying network infrastructure changes frequently, Teleoperation module may require frequent updates (low risk if interface is well-designed).
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module cleanly contains the dual-path failover logic, health monitoring, and availability metric collection. No significant diffusion detected. External interfaces are well-defined and necessary for system integration.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Availability can be verified by continuous monitoring of link state transitions and calculation of uptime ratio. Logging infrastructure must be in place to capture link events with timestamps. Verification is feasible within Teleoperation module's scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-010 is clearly allocated to Teleoperation module. Sub-FRs (link loss detection, safe state transition, redundancy, metric reporting) are also clearly allocated to Teleoperation or Safety Services (for safe state reaction). Allocation is unambiguous.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-A to specify failover latency budget, health check frequency, and path independence criteria.
Establish interface contract for SafetyAlert (what events trigger alerts, alert format, response expectations).
Define availability metric logging format and retention policy.
Confirm Network Link interface ownership and SLA with infrastructure provider.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-010 is not atomic; it decomposes into four sub-FRs addressing link loss detection, safe state transition, redundancy, and metric reporting. Each sub-FR requires further refinement and DP selection. DP-A is sound and well-allocated to Teleoperation, but the sub-FRs must be decomposed to the next level to ensure complete coverage of the availability requirement.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-010.1: The teleoperation system shall detect link loss within [X] milliseconds.
FR-NGHE-010.2: The teleoperation system shall initiate safe state transition upon link loss.
FR-NGHE-010.3: The teleoperation system shall maintain network redundancy to achieve ≥0.999 availability.
FR-NGHE-010.4: The teleoperation system shall log and report link availability metrics to the Safety Services module.

assumptions: Two independent network paths can be provisioned at the worksite.
Automatic failover logic can be implemented with <100ms switchover latency.
Link health monitoring overhead is acceptable within telemetry bandwidth budget.
Availability is measured as uptime ratio over the agreed evaluation period (e.g., 30 days).
Link loss is defined as inability to transmit/receive data for >X milliseconds (threshold to be defined).
open_risks: Simultaneous failure of both network paths; mitigation: redundancy analysis and path independence verification required.
Failover logic bugs causing false positives; mitigation: extensive testing and validation required.
Availability metric logging data volume; mitigation: log rotation and compression strategies required.
Network infrastructure ownership and SLA clarity; mitigation: establish clear interface contract with infrastructure provider.
evidence_needed: Worksite network topology and carrier availability (to confirm dual-path feasibility).
Failover latency measurements from prototype or similar system.
Link health monitoring overhead analysis (bandwidth and CPU impact).
Availability metric logging volume estimates and storage requirements.
SafetyAlert interface specification and Safety Services integration plan.
Network Link interface specification and ownership agreement.
